texto: I may say that this is the greatest factor: the way in which the expedition is equipped, the way in which every difficulty is foreseen, and precautions taken for meeting or avoiding it. Victory awaits him who has everything in order, luck, people call it. Defeat is certain for him who has neglected to take the necessary precautions in time, this is called bad luck.
explicacao: Citação de Roald Amundsen sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “I may say that this is the greatest factor: the way in which the expedition is equipped, the way …” articula motivacao, exploration, inspiracional em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e inspiracional.

frase_original: I may say that this is the greatest factor: the way in which the expedition is equipped, the way in which every difficulty is foreseen, and precautions taken for meeting or avoiding it. Victory awaits him who has everything in order, luck, people call it. Defeat is certain for him who has neglected to take the necessary precautions in time, this is called bad luck.

texto: We must always remember with gratitude and admiration the first sailors who steered their vessels through storms and mists, and increased our knowledge of the lands of ice in the South.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Roald Amundsen sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. A afirmação “We must always remember with gratitude and admiration the first sailors who steered their vessels…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sabedoria, gratitude, remember, útil em momentos de dia-chuvoso e remember.
